response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot engineer with genuine low-latency, high-throughput systems experience, but his profile does not match this role's core requirement: hands-on C++ depth. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, and the last decade has centered on TypeScript/Node web platforms. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, or OT protocols, all of which are central to this position, and the current unexplained career gap adds minor uncertainty. The systems skills are transferable in principle, but the gap to 'expert C++ developer in industrial cybersecurity' is too wide for a senior-manager-level hire.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency async I/O services handling millions of requests per second (DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring, workshops, and open-source maintainership
Event-driven and distributed-systems exposure through his Geo-Data platform and microservices work, with Node.js/React skills matching the 'advantageous' list
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (roughly 7 months, ending mid-2015); STL/modern C++ depth required by the role is unevidenced
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or OT-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA) background
Recent decade is web/full-stack focused, and an unexplained ~9-month career gap precedes the application
